nova_distribution.note: BSIP1 nova assignment. Engine may reclassify at BSIP2 level — batch summary shows different distribution due to routing. BSIP1 is the authoritative NOVA assignment.

d4_additive_wiring.acceptance_rationale: Israeli juice brands under-represented in OFF. Juice ingredient lists structurally simple (1-3 ingredients). Documented in RT-J-008 and W-JUICE-008.

frontier_findings: Only 1/65 products reaches A — fresh-squeezed (NOVA 1 floor). No B grades. Structural compression at C/D reflects juice category physiology: zero fiber, zero satiety, significant sugar load.
100% juice can contain 2x the sugar of the lowest-sugar nectar (lemon 1.75g vs. grape 16.8g) — the sub-pool label alone does not predict nutritional quality.
Nectars with 'no added sugar' label using fruit concentrate as sweetener cannot claim sugar-free — concentrate sugar counts in full. Engine correctly counts total sugar.
Cold-pressed process does not reduce sugar content. Three cold-pressed products score C (54-57) despite NOVA 1 assignment — processing class does not override sugar density for beverages.
Muscat grape juice misrouted to dessert archetype (engine gap: 'מוסקט' token). Score directionally plausible (57.4/C). Flagged for future router update.

ambiguous_classification_edge_cases.name: סחוט תפוזים 1
ambiguous_classification_edge_cases.issue: Fresh-squeezed in juice_100 pool. Receives A via NOVA 1 floor. Concentrate-based 100% juice scores C. Both are 'juice_100' by label, but 23-31 pt gap driven by processing class.

ambiguous_classification_edge_cases.name: נקטר ספרינג תות
ambiguous_classification_edge_cases.issue: OFF record in French — ingredient text in French (Jus de pomme 16%, Purée de fraises 8%). Classified as nectar correctly based on fruit content pct (30-50% estimated).
ambiguous_classification_edge_cases.resolution: French ingredient text parsed for D4 detection. No E-numbers detected. Classified nectar.

ambiguous_classification_edge_cases.name: מיץ ענבים תירוש
ambiguous_classification_edge_cases.issue: Grape tiroush (100% grapes) has 16.8g sugar/100ml — highest in category. Correctly in juice_100 but creates wide intra-pool sugar range.
ambiguous_classification_edge_cases.resolution: Accepted as honest finding. sugarPer100ml surfaced in frontend.

warnings: W-JUICE-001: 86% FDC generic references — scores directional, not SKU-specific. All stamped candidate.
W-JUICE-002: Muscat grape juice misrouted (dessert archetype). Annotated in frontend.
W-JUICE-003: Cold-pressed NOVA 1 but C score — expected. insightLine explains.
W-JUICE-004: Fresh-squeezed sole A. Score verified against trace.
W-JUICE-008: D4 ingredient coverage 4.6% — below 15% gate. Accepted (structural juice data gap).
